1 Landing — Social Proof Scan
interest Confidence 0.70

"Alright, TypingMind -- 'The best frontend for LLMs.' Bold claim. I see 20,641+ users, 5-star ratings on Capterra, Product of the Day on Product Hunt, 4.5 on G2, 4.6 on TrustPilot. Solid social proof across the board. 50% off lifetime deal -- that's aggressive but attention-grabbing. Let me close this popup and actually explore the product."

⚠ Marketing popup on first load blocks immediate product exploration
2 Main Chat Interface
interest Confidence 0.70

"Clean interface after dismissing all that. GPT-5.4 already selected as the default -- they keep up with the latest models, good sign. I see a text input, model selector dropdown, and a bunch of icons in the sidebar: what looks like chats, agents, prompts, plugins... The left sidebar has a full icon rail. But the first thing I need to know is: what models do I get access to? My team needs ChatGPT AND Claude AND Gemini. Let me click this model dropdown..."

✓ Clean, functional interface immediately after dismissing popup — professional impression
3 Model Selector — Multi-Model
anticipation Confidence 0.75

"Oh nice -- GPT-5.4 with 1M context, Mini, Nano, Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite, Claude Sonnet 4.6 with 200K context, Gemini 3.1 Pro with 1M... and custom models for Llama, Mistral, Perplexity. All in one dropdown. That's exactly what I need -- one interface, all the major models. Context windows are clearly labeled too. This is already way better than juggling three separate subscriptions. My team could switch between Claude for writing, GPT for coding, Gemini for research, all without leaving the app."

✓ All major models in one dropdown — directly solves team's multi-subscription pain
4 Pricing — $99 Lifetime Sticker
anticipation Confidence 0.80

"Hold on -- $99 lifetime for Premium? Was $198, now 50% off. That's absurdly cheap. My team's paying $220 a MONTH right now for just ChatGPT and Claude. Even if I bought 12 individual Premium licenses, that's $1,188 one-time versus $2,640 per year. The catch: 'price does not include API costs.' So the license is just the frontend, and I pay OpenAI/Anthropic directly for API usage. That's the BYOK model -- same thing JetBrains and GitHub Copilot do. Smart business model. Let me scroll to see team options..."

⚠ API cost disclaimer hidden in fine print — BYOK TCO is unclear for non-technical decision makers
5 Testimonials — Trust Validation
trust Confidence 0.80

"Let me scan these testimonials quickly. Niels -- 'cost effective to pay by token rather than monthly subscription,' that validates the BYOK model. Thomas Frank with 3M YouTube subs -- 'entirely switched from normal ChatGPT client.' Graydon, founder of IT services -- 'centralize all interactions with different models, no reason to use any other application.' Shavin, Product Manager -- 'our team uses it every day, 10/10.' These aren't fake reviews, they're linked to Capterra, TrustPilot, Product Hunt. Let me find the team pricing..."

✓ Founder and PM testimonials on verified platforms confirm team adoption credibility
6 Team Options — Bulk vs Teams
interest Confidence 0.82

"Two team options. Bulk License: $395 for 10 users on 50 devices, Premium plan benefits at half price. That's $39.50 per person one-time. But I have 12 people -- capped at 10. Do I buy two bulk licenses? That's messy. TypingMind for Teams: 'custom, private AI chat portal with easy member management.' That sounds more like what I need for a real company deployment -- centralized admin, probably usage controls. Let me check Team pricing..."

⚠ Bulk License capped at 10 — no clear path for 11–25 person teams
7 TypingMind Teams Landing
anticipation Confidence 0.85

"TypingMind Teams -- this is a whole separate product! 'Build a unified AI workspace for your team.' Custom domain at ai.company.com -- that's slick, I could brand it as an internal tool. 'Trusted by Fortune 500 Companies' -- if Devon Energy and StoryTel are using it, the security and compliance story should be solid. Centralized, secure, private. Self-host option too for companies that need it. This feels like a real enterprise product, not just a ChatGPT wrapper. Let me check Pricing..."

⚠ Personal and Teams products on separate domains — jarring context switch
8 Teams Pricing — Annual Plans
joy Confidence 0.90

"NOW we're talking. Four tiers -- Starter $83/month for 5 seats with $8/extra seat, Growth $166/month, Professional $249/month at 50% off from $499, Business custom starting $7K/year. Let me do the math: Starter with 12 seats = $83 + (7 x $8) = $139/month. That's less than our current $220/month! And we get ALL models instead of just two. Professional at $249/month adds analytics, audit logs, RBAC, SSO -- that's what a real team deployment needs. But that's more than our current spend. Key question: what are the actual API costs on top? If my team's usage patterns are similar, API could run $50-100/month. So total $139-349/month depending on tier... versus $220/month for just subscriptions with rate limits and no analytics."

⚠ No ROI calculator on Teams pricing — decision makers can't show exact savings to board
9 Teams Pricing — Monthly Toggle
joy Confidence 0.88

"Monthly prices -- Starter $99, Growth $199, Professional $299. Annual saves about 16-17%. For my team, Starter monthly at $99 + 7 extra seats at $8 = $155/month. That's $30% less than our current $220/month, and we gain access to ALL models. If I commit annually, Starter is $139/month -- 37% savings. Professional at $299/month (monthly) or $249/month (annual) is more expensive, but I get SSO for future scaling, analytics to track ROI quarterly (which I already do for all tools), and audit logs for compliance. I'd start with Starter, prove the ROI to the team in Q2, then upgrade to Professional in Q3. 14-day free trial makes this a no-brainer to test."

✓ 14-day free trial de-risks the decision — 'no-brainer to test' for CEO persona
10 AI Agents — Killer Feature
joy Confidence 0.92

"AI Agents -- this is the killer feature for a team. I can create pre-built agents for specific tasks with: Assigned Plugins, Model & Parameters, Custom Instructions, Training Files, Knowledge Base access, Dynamic Context. This is essentially a no-code agent builder. I could set up a 'Sales Assistant' agent that knows our product docs, a 'Code Reviewer' for engineering, a 'Customer Support' bot with our FAQ knowledge base... each with the optimal model and parameters. ChatGPT Team doesn't let me do this without Enterprise at $25/user/month. This is way cheaper and more flexible. I've seen enough -- starting the free trial tonight."

✓ AI Agents with role-specific knowledge bases is the decisive team adoption feature
